payload:
SOLE Research Loop — self-organizing experiment pipeline.

ARCHITECTURE: SOLE (Structurally Orthogonal Latent Experts). All-modules LoRA, rank-16.
STATE: Read .ralph/current_direction.md for active work. Read HYPOTHESES.yml for full roadmap.
CONTEXT: See VISION.md (architecture), FINDINGS.md (proven results).

HOW TO PICK WORK:
1. Read HYPOTHESES.yml
2. Find all `open` nodes whose `depends_on` are ALL satisfied (status `proven` or `supported`)
3. If your prompt specifies a SCALE CONSTRAINT (e.... [truncated, 2320 chars total]
exp_reasoning_expert_distillation: P1 macro, SPEC at micro/models/reasoning_expert_distillation/SPEC.md. 3 chained scripts ready for GPU submission. Blocks 2 downstream nodes.

task_id: run_reasoning_distillation_1773616066, experiment: exp_reasoning_expert_distillation, script: run_reasoning_distillation.py (chains train+eval_math500+eval_interference), queue_position: 8th (7 pending ahead), completed_tasks: composition_quality(OK) kl_health_x2(OK) kl_health_x2(FAILED)

no_new_results: all 5 completed tasks already harvested. active: run_humaneval_only (20h+, GPU 36% - possible slow sequential gen). pending: 7 tasks (orthogonality x2, clone_compete, leave_one_out, dropout_robustness, dynamic_weight, reasoning_distillation)
no_new_completions: humaneval still active (22h+, GPU 38%, sequential generation of 328 problems). 7 pending tasks blocked. checkpointing enabled so progress preserved. will re-check.
harvested: orthogonality (KILLED, caveats), humaneval (FAILED, salvaged). clone_compete now ACTIVE, 6 pending. queue healthy.
no new completions. clone_compete active (GPU 7%), 6 pending. queue healthy, re-check later.
no new completions. clone_compete active 17hrs (GPU 8%), 6 pending. queue healthy, re-check later.
no new completions. clone_compete active 41hrs (GPU 12%), 6 pending. if still running at 48hrs, consider killing. queue healthy.
no new completions. clone_compete still active (GPU 7%), approaching 48hr kill threshold. 6 pending blocked. queue healthy.
no new completions. clone_compete still active (GPU 7%), approaching 48hr kill threshold. 6 pending blocked. queue healthy.

no new completions. clone_compete still active (GPU 7%, 6.6GB VRAM), check #9. FINAL PATIENCE CHECK — if still 7% at check #10, kill and resubmit with per-domain checkpointing + timeout. 6 pending blocked. queue healthy.

check #10 DONE. Killed hung clone_compete (41hrs, log frozen at bash FT step 32/50). Fixed: added per-domain checkpointing + 30min timeout + 2hr max runtime. Resubmitted. Also fixed humaneval_v2 (load_in_4bit→precision bf16). Queue unblocked: 7 pending (leave_one_out, dropout, dynamic_weight, reasoning, clone_compete_v2, humaneval_v3). Worker healthy, processing.

check #11 DONE. Worker recovered, processing normally. leave_one_out now ACTIVE (just started). 5 pending (dropout, dynamic_weight, reasoning, clone_compete_v2, humaneval_v3). Duplicate orthogonality completed (no new data). eval_humaneval_v2 still used broken flag, fixed resubmission pending. No new results to harvest.

check #12 DONE. No new completed tasks. leave_one_out ACTIVE (GPU 0%, 14.5GB VRAM — model loaded, likely CPU-bound phase). 5 pending (dropout, dynamic_weight, reasoning, clone_compete_v2, humaneval_v3). Queue healthy.

check #13 DONE. No new completed tasks. leave_one_out ACTIVE (GPU 0%, 14.5GB VRAM — model loaded, CPU-bound eval phase). 5 pending (dropout, dynamic_weight, reasoning, clone_compete_v2, humaneval_v3). Queue healthy.
check #14: RunPod pod DOWN (SSH timeout). leave_one_out ACTIVE status unknown. 5 pending tasks stalled. Pod needs restart via gpu_queue.py start before queue can resume. Budget ~$30 remaining.

check #15: RunPod pod STILL DOWN. Cannot restart programmatically — no runpodctl, no pod ID. USER MUST restart pod manually via RunPod web dashboard, then run gpu_queue.py start. 6 GPU tasks blocked (leave_one_out active/unknown, 5 pending). Budget ~$30 remaining.
check #16: RunPod pod STILL DOWN (3rd consecutive). 6 GPU tasks blocked. User must restart pod manually. Consider pivoting to CPU-only micro experiments while waiting.
RunPod pod DOWN for 4+ checks. Pivoting to CPU-only micro experiments. Pick lowest-priority open micro node from HYPOTHESES.yml. GPU queue has 6 pending tasks that will auto-resume when pod restarts.

exp_relora_merge_cycle_scaling: K1 SURVIVES (cos_ratio=4.58 at K=200, threshold >5.0), K2 SURVIVES (loss_ratio=1.167, threshold >1.50). Composition penalty stable ~1-2% across all K. ReLoRA base safe for production SOLE. PAPER.md complete at micro/models/relora_merge_cycle_scaling/PAPER.md

exp_relora_merge_cycle_scaling: supported. Composition penalty stable 1-2% across K=5..200, driven by base quality not merge geometry. Caveats: cos_ratio noisy, K/T_c confound, FFN-only. Base-freedom readiness 40%->45%.

exp_minimum_viable_base: supported. All-modules LoRA composition geometrically safe at all model sizes (d=64 to d=3584). K1 killed (even micro d has max|cos|=0.007). Phase transition at d=256. Minimum viable base is about capability not geometry.

exp_minimum_viable_base: REVISE. 4 fixes required: (1) K1 kill criteria is category error—experiment measures geometry not PPL, change to NOT TESTABLE; (2) N_max analytical estimates unvalidated, qualify as extrapolations; (3) Add random baseline comparison to show whether Stiefel/domain-B structure matters vs plain random vectors; (4) Tone down phase transition language—slope change of 0.021 is noise, use saturation/diminishing returns.

exp_minimum_viable_base revision complete. 4 required fixes applied: (1) K1→NOT TESTABLE, (2) N_max qualified as extrapolation, (3) random baseline added—LoRA cosines within 5-13% of random vectors proving dimensionality drives orthogonality not structure, (4) phase transition→saturation language. Runtime 193s.

exp_minimum_viable_base supported. |cos|~1/sqrt(D_flat) confirmed (beta=-0.506, R2=0.997). Random baseline proves orthogonality from dimensionality not structure. K1 NOT TESTABLE. All-modules > FFN-only at every d.

exp_decorrelation_filter_scaling KILLED (both K1+K2). Filter ratio INCREASES with d (d^+0.603), not decreases. At d>=256 trained>random. Decorrelation at d=64 was artifact of high random baseline, not structural property. SOLE safety comes from concentration of measure, not Grassmannian decorrelation. Absolute cosines remain tiny (<0.002) at all d.

exp_decorrelation_filter_scaling KILLED (adversarial review confirmed). Both K1+K2 triggered. Filter ratio increases with d (d^+0.603), not decreases. SOLE safety from concentration of measure, not decorrelation. consecutive_kills=1.

exp_execution_based_self_learning SUPPORTED (micro simulation). K1 PASS: DPO +22.7pp pass@1 after 5 cycles, SFT +10.8pp. K2 CONDITIONAL: safe 12-26 cycles, collapse above 5-8%/cycle acceleration. DPO >> SFT (+20.8pp peak). K=3 sufficient. 50% fresh data prevents collapse. Simulation calibrated from SPIN/ReST-EM/CodeRL.

exp_execution_based_self_learning: 6 fixes required. (1) Acknowledge DPO 2.1x advantage is parametric input (1.91x from alpha*beta), not emergent. (2) Reconcile two divergent diversity models between main script (uniformity-weighted) and stress test (clean accelerating). (3) Note near-zero Monte Carlo variance in collapse cycles — 20 seeds add no info. (4) Remove/qualify circular fresh data prediction (sim uses same r=0.02). (5) Fix diversity_decay_dpo discrepancy: 0.02 in main vs 0.015 in stress test. (6) Verify K=3 finding under accelerating decay, not just constant.

exp_execution_based_self_learning REVISION complete. All 6 adversarial fixes applied: DPO advantage acknowledged as parametric input (1.91x), diversity models unified (clean geometric decay), MC variance explained as deterministic, fresh data reframed as self-consistency, gamma_DPO unified to 0.015, K sweep under accelerating decay confirms K=3 optimal. New insight: K does not affect collapse timing. Results: DPO +23.0pp, SFT +11.2pp, advantage 2.06x. K1 PASS, K2 CONDITIONAL unchanged.

exp_execution_based_self_learning SUPPORTED. DPO +23.0pp (SFT +11.2pp), K=3 optimal, collapse mitigable with 50% fresh data. Golden window 10-25 cycles. Adversarial review PROCEED (revision 2). consecutive_kills reset to 0.

exp_model_collapse_detection SUPPORTED. LoRA rank is complete natural regularizer: -1.6% diversity loss over 30 cycles at rank-16 (46x margin below 30% kill threshold). Full-rank collapses at 73% loss in 5 cycles. Rank value irrelevant (rho=0.000). No fresh data needed under LoRA constraint. Resolves parent limitations #7 and #8.

exp_model_collapse_detection REVISE: 5 fixes required. (1) Add anchored full-rank baseline to isolate rank effect from base-anchoring. (2) Add LoRA ablation without norm constraint to disentangle rank from norm-bounding. (3) Soften no-fresh-data claim to conjecture. (4) Reframe correlation experiment as uninformative under LoRA constraint. (5) Fix incorrect MATH.md matrix norm bound. See REVIEW-adversarial.md for details.

exp_model_collapse_detection v2 revision complete. All 5 reviewer fixes applied. Key finding refined: rank + norm bounding jointly prevent collapse (neither alone suffices). Anchored full-rank baseline collapses at 100%, confirming rank is the mechanism. Status: SUPPORTED with proper attribution.
exp_model_collapse_detection v2: PROCEED. Rank + norm bounding jointly prevent model collapse (SUPPORTED). Integrated into FINDINGS.md and HYPOTHESES.yml.
exp_individual_expert_held_out: Individual expert held-out MMLU eval. SPEC at macro/individual_expert_held_out/SPEC.md. Diagnoses distillation vs composition interference for -3.67pp regression.

exp=individual_expert_held_out, script=macro/individual_expert_held_out/run_individual_eval.py, status=BLOCKED_RUNPOD_DOWN, detail=RunPod SSH connection refused (203.57.40.222:10254 reset). Script is ready but pod needs restart before submission. Improvements: batched inference bs=16, all 57 MMLU subjects, no max_per_subject cap, bootstrap CI 1000x, in-domain/out-of-domain analysis, crash resilience, SMOKE_TEST support, kill_criteria JSON.

RunPod down (SSH refused). Worker STOPPED, queue empty. exp_individual_expert_held_out script ready at macro/individual_expert_held_out/run_individual_eval.py awaiting pod restart. No results to harvest.
RunPod still down (SSH connection reset 203.57.40.222:10254). Worker STOPPED, queue empty. exp_individual_expert_held_out script ready, awaiting pod restart. Human intervention needed to restart pod.

RunPod still down (SSH connection reset, check #3). Worker STOPPED, queue empty. exp_individual_expert_held_out script ready at macro/individual_expert_held_out/run_individual_eval.py. Blocked on human restarting pod.

RunPod still down (check #4, SSH connection reset 203.57.40.222:10254). Worker STOPPED, queue empty. exp_individual_expert_held_out script ready. RECOMMEND: pivot to micro/CPU work while waiting for pod restart.

RunPod down 5th consecutive check. Worker STOPPED, queue empty. exp_individual_expert_held_out script ready but cannot submit. STRONG RECOMMEND: pivot to micro/CPU experiment next iteration. Stop re-checking GPU until human restarts pod.

RunPod down 6 consecutive checks. Worker STOPPED, queue empty. exp_individual_expert_held_out script ready but cannot submit. Pivoting to micro/CPU-scale experiment. Pick lowest-priority open micro node from HYPOTHESES.yml.

exp_taap_convergence: TAAP (Truncated AP) closes gap to Welch bound for large N. Scale: micro. Dir: micro/models/taap_convergence. Dep: grassmannian_expert_init (proven). Kill: TAAP coherence not closer to Welch bound than standard AP; runtime >3x. Standard AP converges 2.8-3x above Welch bound. TAAP truncation discards worst offenders. Important for N=500+.

max_runtime
